title: Policy Framework for Salmonella Control in Ready-to-Eat Foods
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Ready-  to  -  eat   (RTE)   foods   are   a     growing     segment     of     the   global   food     market  ,   offering     convenience     but     also     presenting     elevated     risks     of     Salmonella     contamination     due     to   minimal   or     no     heat     treatment     before     consumption  .   Salmonella     is     one     of     the     most     significant     causes     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   leading     to   gastroenteritis,   hospitalizations  ,   and     economic     losses  .   Strengthening     policy     frameworks     for     its     control   in RTE   foods     is     essential     for     safeguarding     public     health  .   This     brief     highlights     the     urgent     need     for     harmonized     microbiological     criteria  ,   robust     surveillance     systems  ,   and     risk-based     regulations     to     mitigate     contamination  .   Emphasizing     collaboration     between     government  ,   industry  ,   and     laboratories  ,   it     provides     a     roadmap     for     comprehensive     prevention     strategies     that     protect     consumers  ,   enhance     trade     opportunities  ,   and     reduce     the     overall     burden     of     Salmonella  -related     illnesses  .
